quote: Please leave your message for John Livingston.
stewart_read: The call reached voicemail. No live interaction occurred. There is no rep behavior to evaluate — strategic or reactive. The only observable fact is that the rep either hung up without leaving a message or the recording captured nothing after the tone.
coaching_implication: Manager should confirm with the rep: was a voicemail message left? Ion's callback and voicemail protocol is not defined in the current schema (TBD section). This call surfaces the gap — the floor needs a standard for what setters do on voicemail connects: leave a message or not, and if so, what script. Without a defined protocol, reps are improvising on every no-answer, which creates inconsistent lead follow-up and potential compliance exposure.

stewart_read: The customer's name is confirmed as John Livingston from the voicemail greeting. This is the only qualifying data point the transcript yields. No bill, no address, no title, no interest signal — nothing from the verify section was captured.
coaching_implication: If this is a paid lead, the bill is still required before an appointment can be set (context.lead_types_and_bill_collection_rules). The rep needs a callback strategy that re-engages John and moves through the verify section efficiently. Manager should check whether a callback was logged in CRM.

stewart_read: The voicemail system confirms no message was left. This is a dead-end touch with no follow-up artifact — no voicemail, no text, no CRM note visible in the transcript. The lead is effectively in limbo unless the rep took action outside this recording.
coaching_implication: This is the exact scenario the schema's proposed automated bill-capture and follow-up flow is designed to address (bill_collection.stewart_automated_bill_capture). Until that capability ships, the rep needs a manual protocol: log the attempt, send an introductory SMS if permitted, schedule a callback at a different time of day. Manager should verify the rep completed any of these steps.

quote: Simply hang up when done. Or for delivery options, press the pound sign.
stewart_read: The transcript ends at the voicemail system's closing prompt. No rep action is captured. This call yields zero coaching signal on setter technique — it is a routing and protocol gap, not a performance gap.
coaching_implication: The manager's time on this call should be spent on one question: does the floor have a defined voicemail protocol? If not, this call is a prompt to build one — what to say, how long, what CRM action follows, and whether SMS outreach is permitted on this lead type. That protocol question is more valuable than any rep-specific coaching this transcript could support.
